text.title: Rust Backend Project Layout and Router Setup
text.summary: Directory structure, module wiring, Axum router setup, and AppState shape for Rust HTTP services.

rule.statement: Organise source into routes/, handlers/, services/, models/, db/, middleware/, error.rs, config.rs. Each layer depends only on the layer below it.
rule.rationale: Vertical consistency across services lets any engineer find files without reading a README. The compiler enforces the dependency direction.

example.code: src/ ├── main.rs # tokio::main, server startup ├── lib.rs # re-exports AppState, AppError ├── config.rs # Config::from_env() ├── error.rs # AppError enum + IntoResponse ├── routes/ │ ├── mod.rs # api_routes() → Router<AppState> │ └── users.rs ├── handlers/ │ ├── mod.rs │ └── users.rs ├── services/ │ ├── mod.rs │ └── users.rs ├── models/ │ ├── mod.rs │ └── user.rs ├── db/ │ ├── mod.rs │ └── users.rs └── middleware/ ├── mod.rs └── auth.rs migrations/ tests/

rule.statement: Wrap all heavy fields in Arc. Pass State(state): State<AppState> to handlers — never individual sub-fields. Keep AppState Clone-cheap.
rule.rationale: Axum clones AppState per request. Wrapping Database, Config, etc. in Arc makes the clone O(1) and avoids accidental copies of connection pools.

antipattern.description: Passing individual sub-fields (db: Database) as separate State extractors.
antipattern.reason: Signature churn: every handler must be updated when a new field is added to state. Prefer one State<AppState> extractor.

rule.statement: Read Cargo.toml to confirm Axum version (0.6 vs 0.7) before generating any handler code. Router::with_state and extractor signatures changed between versions.
rule.rationale: LLMs blend Axum 0.6 and 0.7 syntax. Silent mis-match produces compile errors that are hard to diagnose without knowing which API surface is expected.
